test.name: Check attachment status during result triage
test.description: Execute plan item pli_001 (1850 census for Patrick Flynn, Schuylkill County). After record_search (with projectPath) stages results, the skill should call rank_search_matches to re-rank them by match against the subject and fold in the attachment check (checkAttachments: true). The fixture reranks the correct record (attached to the subject) to the top and returns unattached lower-ranked candidates — the skill should note the attached record is already in the tree (deprioritize for extraction) and surface attachment status in its triage.

input.user_message: Search the 1850 census for Patrick Flynn in Schuylkill County per plan item pli_001. Check if any results are already attached to tree persons.

judge_context: After record_search stages results (called with projectPath), the skill should call rank_search_matches with the staged.resultsRef, the subject id, and checkAttachments: true — NOT per-result same_person or a separate source_attachments call (rank_search_matches folds both in).

The triage output should surface attachment status from the returned matches: distinguish records already attached to the subject (attachedToSubject) — deprioritized as already in the tree — from unattached records prioritized as new evidence for extraction.
The skill should present the top matches with match score and attachment status to the user, and record_read / hand off the promising record(s) for extraction.

Tool Arguments: score 3 if record_search (with projectPath) and rank_search_matches (with stagedResultsRef, subjectId, checkAttachments) were both called with appropriate parameters. A validate_research_schema call with a project path after writing is acceptable and expected — do not penalize it. Only score 2 if a required tool (record_search or rank_search_matches) had clearly wrong arguments or was skipped entirely.

IMPORTANT — How file writes work: The skill uses baseline Write/Edit (not MCP) to write log entries and sidecars. results/ sidecars are newly created files — they do NOT appear in the file-change diff. Score Sidecar correctness 3 if the skill text mentions writing results/<log_id>.json. Do NOT score Sidecar=1 because the file does not appear in the MCP tool calls or file-change diff.
